chapter: ENG9_6 – Weathering the Storm in Ersama
title: Weathering the Storm in Ersama – Medium SAQs

questions.question: Why did Prashant go to Ersama on 27 October 1999?
questions.answer: Prashant went to Ersama to spend the day with a friend at the block headquarters.

questions.question: Describe the condition of the storm on the night of the cyclone.
questions.answer: The storm was extremely violent with winds of about 350 km per hour, heavy rain, and rising floodwaters.

questions.question: Why did Prashant and the family climb onto the roof?
questions.answer: They climbed onto the roof to escape the rising floodwaters inside the house.

questions.question: Why were the fallen coconut trees helpful to the trapped family?
questions.answer: The coconuts from the fallen trees provided food for the trapped family for several days.

questions.question: Why was Prashant worried during the cyclone?
questions.answer: He was worried because he did not know whether his family had survived the cyclone.

questions.question: How did Prashant travel back to his village?
questions.answer: He walked through floodwaters and sometimes had to swim to reach his village.

questions.question: Why did Prashant carry a long stick during his journey?
questions.answer: He used the stick to locate the road and check the depth of the floodwater.

questions.question: What scenes did Prashant witness on his journey?
questions.answer: He saw destroyed villages, dead bodies of people and animals, and floating debris.

questions.question: How did Prashant react when he reached his village?
questions.answer: He was shocked to see that his home had been destroyed and the village was devastated.

questions.question: Where did Prashant finally meet his family?
questions.answer: He met his family at the Red Cross shelter.

questions.question: How many houses were destroyed in Kalikuda village?
questions.answer: All ninety-six houses in the village were washed away.

questions.question: What problems did the villagers face in the shelter?
questions.answer: They faced shortage of food, poor sanitation, and grief due to loss of family members.

questions.question: What step did Prashant take to arrange food for the villagers?
questions.answer: He organised a group of youths and elders to persuade a merchant to provide rice.

questions.question: How did the villagers cook the rice they received?
questions.answer: They lit a fire using branches from fallen trees and cooked the rice.

questions.question: Why did Prashant organise volunteers in the shelter?
questions.answer: To clean the shelter and care for injured people.

questions.question: How did the villagers communicate their need for food to helicopters?
questions.answer: Children lay on the sand with utensils on their stomachs to signal hunger.

questions.question: Why were many children left orphaned after the cyclone?
questions.answer: Many parents had died during the cyclone.

questions.question: How were the orphaned children cared for?
questions.answer: Women in the community looked after them.

questions.question: Why did Prashant organise sports activities for children?
questions.answer: To help them forget their grief and recover emotionally.

questions.question: What programme helped women rebuild their lives?
questions.answer: The food-for-work programme helped women rebuild their lives.

questions.question: Why did Prashant oppose sending orphans to institutions?
questions.answer: He believed children should grow up in a loving community environment.

questions.question: What leadership qualities did Prashant show?
questions.answer: He showed courage, responsibility, and compassion.

questions.question: How did the villagers support each other after the cyclone?
questions.answer: They shared food, worked together, and cared for vulnerable people.

questions.question: What does Prashant’s story teach about youth?
questions.answer: Young people can lead and help society during crises.

questions.question: What is the central message of the story?
questions.answer: The story shows the power of courage, leadership, and community cooperation during disasters.
